title: Cryptographic Hashes, SHA-2, SHA-3, and Commitment Boundaries · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds hash-purpose and digest verification contract, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of hash-purpose and digest verification contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish preimage, second-preimage, collision, length, domain, and security-strength properties required by a use case.
Select supported SHA-2 or SHA-3 functions and output lengths from protocol, interoperability, performance, compliance, and security needs.
Hash bounded streams incrementally, propagate read errors, freeze canonical byte identity, and compare expected digests safely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Hash Properties
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 6096 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a hash by purpose while rejecting weak algorithms, ambiguous bytes, and unauthenticated digest claims; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Hash Properties.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish preimage, second-preimage, collision, length, domain, and security-strength properties required by a use case. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Hash Properties, keep Crypto Go Hash Properties connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 3381 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a hash by purpose while rejecting weak algorithms, ambiguous bytes, and unauthenticated digest claims; retain exact canonical bytes, purpose, role, version, and artifact identity; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 2003 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a hash by purpose while rejecting weak algorithms, ambiguous bytes, and unauthenticated digest claims; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require an authenticated trusted expected digest or MAC/signature when attackers can modify both data and digest.
